notes:
Type the following in the terminal ... change USERNAME to your username
WINEPREFIX=/home/USERNAME/.steam/steam/steamapps/steamapps/compatdata/213330/pfx wine "/home/USERNAME/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/LEGO Batman 2/Support/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe"
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2400G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: A window flashes up and then closes, game does not start. I have read the other comments on WINEPREFIX, but I am not going to bother with a CLI command to fix a game on my HTPC. I wanted to add this report to show that the issue still persists with Proton 4.2-2 and Mesa 19.
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes

notes: As said before, in order to run the game, you have to open a terminal and run this command to install DirectX: WINEPREFIX=/home/YOUR-USERNAME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/213330/pfx wine "/home/YOUR-USERNAME/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/LEGO Batman 2/Support/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe" (remind to change "YOUR-USERNAME"). On Manjaro, i got an error on the DirectX installation, so, i just installed the 'libopenal' (sudo pacman -S lib32-openal) and tried again. After those tweaks, the game runs flawlessly, very good fps and no audio issues.
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes

notes:
I will not say that THIS game is not playible.
This is playeble.
But, has a BIG VISUAL problem.

The lighting effects are in a frenetic frenzy of color change.
I'm even afraid someone might get a EPILEPSY with this rain of colors jumping on my screen.
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes

notes: Managed to fix the flashing elements in the game by installing dx9 with "protontricks 213330 d3dx9_41" and adding "PROTON_NO_D3D10=1 %command%" to launch options. Game seems to run flawlessly now.
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes

notes:
The way I fixed this was to install wine and run the command
WINEPREFIX=/iscsi/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/213330/pfx wine "/iscsi/SteamLibrary/steamapps/common/LEGO Batman 2/Support/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe"

please notice there is a space between "pfx" and "wine" and then add another space to the location of the DXSETUP.exe file. You must add quotes if there are any spaces in the path. I hope this helps someone. My path will be different from your path. wherever you install you games and whatnot.
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes

notes: Game works very well after installing Directx9 to proton prefix with winetricks. If you have game installed in your default game folder: WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/213330/pfx/ winetricks directx9 If you have game installed in your custom folder, in my case it was (external HDD etc): WINEPREFIX=/mnt/c7ec5d19-bc87-46fc-9c72-f82ed1183cff/Steam/steamapps/compatdata/213330/pfx winetricks directx9 For the second case make sure to find the appropriate path to your external steam folder and it should work! Enjoy :D
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes

notes: It doesn't work out of the box on Proton, you first have to install DirectX9 e.g. with protontricks GAMEID d3dx9 to get it working with WineD3D. I installed additionally D9VK and it worked great too with this DX9 to Vulkan translation, even when testing it with the new ACO compiler.
title: LEGO® Batman™ 2: DC Super Heroes
